easy.companyDescription: A subscription entertainment company trying to move more of its customer experience online.
easy.macroContext: Broadband use, connected devices, and paid digital media habits are all expanding.
easy.situation: Can a newer delivery model rebuild trust quickly enough to justify rising content and product spending?
easy.longCase: If customers accept the newer model, recurring revenue can scale with broadband and connected-device adoption.
easy.shortCase: A recent trust shock can keep churn elevated while content commitments rise faster than the subscriber base.
setupHints: The old delivery model still contributes cash, but the newer model is where the growth case lives.

medium.companyDescription: A consumer company with repeat customer revenue.
medium.macroContext: Households are shifting discretionary spending among a growing set of digital and traditional options.
medium.situation: Can repeat usage recover enough to support spending needed to retain customers?
medium.longCase: Improving retention could make each customer more valuable without a matching rise in acquisition costs.
medium.shortCase: Ongoing commitments and customer switching could keep costs high while revenue growth slows.

hard.macroContext: Consumers are comparing more choices for discretionary spending and recurring services.
hard.situation: Can customer retention improve before the cost of serving and acquiring users becomes more burdensome?
hard.longCase: Better retention could raise the value of the existing customer base over several periods.
hard.shortCase: Higher operating commitments and switching by customers could delay an improvement in results.

reveal.shortText: That was Netflix. The market was skeptical, but streaming adoption accelerated.
reveal.funFact: The company's transition looked risky at the time, but it became one of the defining consumer-tech winners of the decade.
whyItMoved: Subscriber growth improved after the trust reset.
The company became a stronger platform business over time.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: H029 rewrote Medium and Hard after H028 tied recurring consumer media, connected-screen attention, engagement recovery, churn, and fixed content costs to Netflix. Medium peer set: Netflix/Sirius XM/Pandora/Dish. Hard peer set: Sirius XM/Pandora/Dish/Comcast/Time Warner/Netflix; the prose is limited to general retention economics in discretionary services. Easy and shared labels remain unchanged; Medium/Hard Gate 2 results were removed as stale.

prohibited: consumer media recurring-billing plus 2012 engagement-recovery conjunction

guesses.pointingFact: Subscription entertainment shifting online after a trust shock in 2012
Subscription media with recurring billing model
Media company navigating digital delivery shift
Older delivery model still contributing as newer model grows

direction.cue: If customers accept the newer model, recurring revenue can scale with broadband
